topic.name: Tax Credits for Energy and Health
topic.description: Legislation focused on extending tax credits and deductions for renewable energy investments and health insurance premiums.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to issue, and promote the purchase of, Clean Energy Victory Bonds
extend through 2022 the tax credit for investment in solar energy property, geothermal heat pumps, fuel cell property
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a tax deduction for the first $2,000 of premiums paid for health insurance
Reduces the amount of such deduction for a taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $75,000

Document 3: this bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax credit for qualified microturbine property

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving Medicare services, including electronic health records, preventive care, and cost reduction.

Document 1: Medicare Electronic Medication and Safety Protection (E-MEDS) Act of 2007
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make incentive payments for physician use of an electronic prescription drug program
Document 2: Removing Barriers to Colorectal Cancer Screening Act of 2015
waive coinsurance for colorectal cancer screening tests to cover 100% of their cost under Medicare part B
Document 3: Medicare Funding Warning Response Act of 2008
develop and implement a system for encouraging nationwide adoption and use of interoperable electronic health records

topic.name: Education Funding and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial support and resources for education, particularly focusing on teacher retention and student health services.

Document 1: loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan and William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan programs
highly qualified elementary and secondary school teachers who are employed in a teacher shortage area
Document 2: adjust the maximum annual and aggregate amount that a student may borrow under the Federal Perkins Loan program
to account for inflation
Document 3: allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make demonstration grants to states
reduce such ratio in public secondary schools, elementary schools, and kindergarten

topic.name: Tariff Duty Suspensions
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on specific chemicals and materials under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on piperazine co-polymerized copolyamide resin high-temperature melt adhesive pellets.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Paraquat dichloride.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers.

topic.name: Veterans' Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to health care services for veterans, including mental health and disability compensation.

Document 1: amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014 to make a veteran eligible for mental health care at a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility
regardless of the date on which the veteran enrolled in the VA health care system
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs...to ensure that veterans who are eligible for hospital care and medical services through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) have access to at least one full-service VA medical center in the state
Directs the Secretary to report to Congress on compliance with such requirement

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to the exploration, production, and regulation of oil and gas resources in offshore areas, particularly the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Maximize Offshore Resource Exploration Act of 2008
revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands from leasing for oil and natural gas exploration
Document 2: Bringing Reductions to Energy's Airborne Toxic Health Effects Act
amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
Document 3: Outer Continental Shelf Production Incentive Fee Act
direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases on outer Continental Shelf land

topic.name: Immigration and Refugee Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislation related to the admission and protection of refugees and immigrants, particularly from conflict-affected regions.

Document 1: Provides for the permanent resident status adjustment of certain Liberian nationals who were granted, or are eligible to apply for, temporary protected status on or after March 27, 1991.
Document 2: Requires that, before the U.S. refugee admission of a covered alien, the Department of Homeland Security shall ensure that such person has passed a lie detector test; has submitted to DHS biometric identification information.

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight and reporting of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on designating and protecting wilderness areas and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified national forest and Bureau of Land Management (BLM) public land in California as wilderness
Requires such areas to be administered by the Secretary of the Interior or Agriculture
Document 2: Designates specified National Forest System, Bureau of Land Management, and National Park Service lands in West Virginia, Arizona, New Mexico, Oregon, and California as wilderness
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate specified segments of rivers in Oregon and California as wild, scenic, or recreational rivers
Document 3: This bill amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness'

topic.name: Domestic Violence Prevention and Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on preventing domestic violence and supporting victims through grants and educational programs.

Document 1: Amends the Violence against Women Act of 1994 to replace provisions regarding grants to assist children and youth exposed to violence with a Saving Money and Reducing Tragedies through Prevention (SMART Prevention) grant program.
Authorizes the Attorney General to award grants for the purpose of preventing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking.
Document 2: Amends the Victims of Trafficking and Violence Protection Act of 2000 to authorize the Attorney General to make grants to state and local governments for supervised visitation and safe visitation exchange of children and youth in situations involving domestic violence.
Court-related and child protective services workers education on the dynamics of domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking.

topic.name: Healthcare Program Improvements
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing healthcare services and support systems, particularly for vulnerable populations including children, veterans, and college students.

Document 1: 340B Program Improvement and Integrity Act of 2007
expands the drug discount program to allow participation as a covered entity by certain hospitals.
Document 2: Mental Health on Campus Improvement Act
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to eligible institutions of higher education to improve mental and behavioral health services.
Document 3: Services, Education, and Rehabilitation for Veterans Act
authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to states and other entities to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Control
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and control of substances, including marijuana and opioids, to address public health and safety concerns.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that provisions of such Act related to marihuana shall not apply to any person acting in compliance with state laws relating to the production, possession, distribution, dispensation, administration, or delivery of marijuana.
Document 2: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA), within 90 days, to take such actions as may be necessary to modify the approval of, and limit any subsequent approval of, any drug containing controlled-release oxycodone hychloride to use for the relief of severe-only pain instead of moderate-to-severe pain.
Document 3: This bill amends the Controlled Substances Act to add certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I.

topic.name: Water Rights and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on water rights agreements, reclamation projects, and infrastructure development for water management.

Document 1: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility.
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior
to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.

topic.name: Homeowner Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: A collection of laws aimed at protecting homeowners from foreclosure and ensuring proper notification and assistance during financial distress.

Document 1: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to eliminate the pre-petition credit counseling requirement for a debtor under chapter 13 who is facing foreclosure
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders in the event of a foreclosure notice for a chapter 13 debtor
Document 2: Requires certain notifications if, in connection with any major disaster, the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development makes available forbearance relief
Directs the applicable agency heads to issue guidelines regarding measures designed to provide notice and awareness of forbearance relief
Document 3: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 to require the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to require the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation to charge a guarantee fee
Directs the Director to require each GSE to increase its guarantee fee gradually over two years

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation, research, and sustainability of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Defines 'coral reef ecosystem' as the system of coral reefs and geographically associated species, habitats, and environment.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing financial assistance and support to agricultural producers and rural entities for energy efficiency and disaster recovery.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
to make interest-free loans to eligible entities for energy efficient measures.
Document 2: make a supplemental direct commodity payment to eligible producers
provide supplemental assistance to eligible producers and first-handlers of the 2009 crop of cottonseed in disaster counties.

topic.name: Advanced Vehicle Technologies
topic.description: Research and development initiatives focused on advanced vehicle technologies, including hybrid and natural gas vehicles.

Document 1: establish a competitive research, development, demonstration, and commercial application program
advanced heavy duty hybrid vehicles
Document 2: conduct a five-year program of natural gas vehicle research, development, and demonstration
development of new, cleaner, more efficient light-, medium-, and heavy-duty natural gas vehicle engines

topic.name: Data Privacy Protection Laws
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding personally identifiable information from unauthorized disclosure and ensuring proper notification in case of data breaches.

Document 1: Requires commercial entities to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data in electronic form containing personal information.
Directs a covered entity that owns or licenses such data to give notice of any breach of security that the entity reasonably believes has caused or will cause identity theft.
Document 2: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information unless the disclosure of such information is authorized by law.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.

Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record if such information includes the name, social security number, street address, telephone number, or electronic mail address.

topic.name: Taxpayer Protection and Compliance
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud, protecting taxpayer information, and ensuring compliance with tax obligations.

Document 1: prohibits any federal officer or employee from hiring any Internal Revenue Service (IRS) employees until the IRS submits to Congress
seriously delinquent tax debt is an outstanding tax debt for which the IRS has filed a notice of lien in public records
Document 2: establish a safe harbor rule for errors on tax information
impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity
Document 3: issue uniform standards for the method for truncation of Social Security account numbers
prohibit the display of Social Security account numbers to the general public on the Internet

topic.name: Banking Regulation Reforms
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the activities and ownership structures of financial institutions to ensure stability and prevent conflicts of interest.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Prohibits a national banking association from investing in structured or synthetic products.
Document 2: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Suspends automatically the duty of a savings and loan holding company to file supplementary and periodic information if the securities of each class to which the registration statement relates are held of record by fewer than 1,200 persons.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to authorize any depository institution to lease an interest in residential property which was security for an extension of credit.
Preempts any federal or state law that would restrict the time during which a depository institution may hold or lease property.

topic.name: Recreational Use of Public Lands
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting and regulating recreational activities such as fishing, hunting, and shooting on federal public lands.

Document 1: facilitate the use of, and access to, federal public lands for fishing, sport hunting, and recreational shooting
open to recreational fishing, hunting, and shooting unless the managing agency acts to close such lands
Document 2: expected increases in recreational hunting, fishing, shooting, and conservation activities
authorize a state to pay up to 90% of the costs of acquiring land for, expanding, or constructing a public target range

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at environmental protection, conservation of natural resources, and the designation of wilderness areas.

Document 1: Amends the Washington State Wilderness Act of 1984
prohibits the Secretary of Agriculture from moving the Lookout
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
revises the Lake Pontchartrain Basin Restoration Program
Document 3: Designates certain federal land in Oregon as the Devil's Staircase Wilderness
amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate Franklin and Wasson Creeks as wild rivers

topic.name: Rail Infrastructure and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving rail infrastructure investment and ensuring competitive practices among rail carriers.

Document 1: allow a tax credit for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property
extend through 2012 the tax credit for qualified railroad track maintenance expenditures
Document 2: Sets forth Surface Transportation Board directives calling for effective competition among rail carriers
prohibits the Board from issuing a certificate authorizing construction and operation of railroad lines

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Education and Technology
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing education, technology transfer, and the peaceful use of nuclear energy.

Document 1: U.S. policy to support the establishment of an international regime for the assured supply of nuclear fuel for peaceful means
requires compliance with IAEA safeguards
Document 2: establishes the Commission on American Discoveries and American Jobs to study technology transfer
potential benefits of reinvesting revenues into STEM education
Document 3: supports modernization of facilities for science, technology, engineering, mathematics, or career and technical education
providing grants or loan guarantees for educational facility improvements

topic.name: Tax and Economic Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation providing tax relief and incentives for individuals affected by disasters and modifications to retirement and health care plans.

Document 1: tax-free distributions from retirement plans for individuals in affected states
tax incentives for home purchases
Document 2: modifies the definition of governmental plan for Indian tribal pension plans
Document 3: repeals restrictions on health savings accounts
allows payments for over-the-counter medications

topic.name: Small Business Financing Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing financing options and support for small businesses through loan guarantees and investment programs.

Document 1: Dealer Floor Plan Program Extension and Improvement Act of 2010
SBA loan guarantees to assist dealers of titleable assets to finance inventory.
Document 2: Helping Small Business Succeed Act of 2009
increase maximum amounts of loans under the section 7(a) guaranteed loan program.
Document 3: Small Business Investment Company Modernization Act of 2013
increase the maximum amount of outstanding leverage to be made available by the SBA.

topic.name: Tribal Rights and Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on the recognition, rights, and services for Native American tribes and their members.

Document 1: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990
authorizes appropriations for child care workforce development initiatives.
Document 2: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government.
Document 3: increases the maximum term of imprisonment from 5 years to 10 years for individuals convicted more than once of illegal trafficking in Native American human remains or cultural items
bans the export of illegally obtained Native American cultural objects.

topic.name: Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving and adjusting benefits and protections for elderly individuals under Social Security and related programs.

Document 1: establish in the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund a Social Security Surplus Protection Account
Social Security Investment Commission to study the most effective vehicles for investment
Document 2: prepare and publish a monthly Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers
computation of cost-of-living increases for Social Security and Medicare benefits
Document 3: restrict the application of the windfall elimination requirement
provide for a graduated implementation of such requirement

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks and Health Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to regulatory improvements and public health initiatives, particularly in the context of consumer safety and economic impacts.

Document 1: Skin Cancer Prevention, Education, and Consumer Right-To-Know Act
nationwide education campaign identifying the risks posed by sun exposure
Document 2: Regulatory Flexibility Improvements Act of 2011
initial and final regulatory flexibility analyses
Document 3: Smarter Regulations Through Advance Planning and Review Act of 2015
framework for assessing the rule

topic.name: Sanctions and Nonproliferation Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and preventing the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, particularly in relation to Iran, North Korea, and Syria.

Document 1: Directs the President to identify in the Federal Register and apply specified sanctions against a foreign person or foreign entity that is a representative or affiliate of the Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
Excludes such persons from U.S. entry.
Document 2: Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds made available to the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), the Department of Defense (DOD), or any other U.S. agency or entity involved in intelligence activities for the purpose of supporting military or paramilitary operations in Syria.
Directs the Secretary of State to report every 90 days on assistance provided to groups, organizations, movements, and individuals in Syria.
Document 3: Directs the President to impose sanctions for not less than two years on any foreign person who on or after September 1, 2007, transferred to or acquired from Iran, Syria, or North Korea certain listed nuclear, dual use, missile, chemical, biological, toxic, or nonlisted but otherwise prohibited goods, services, or technology.
Applies the following sanctions to such prohibited activities: arms export prohibitions, Executive Order 12938 prohibitions, dual use export prohibitions.

topic.name: Housing Policy and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating housing assistance and conditions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to extend the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
Makes any area eligible for rural housing programs authorized under the Act on June 30, 2012, eligible for such programs through FY2013.
Document 2: Amends the Quality Housing and Work Responsibility Act of 1998 to replace the definition of 'drug-related criminal activity' with 'covered criminal activity'.

Requires a PHA or owner, when screening of applicants for federally assisted housing, to conduct an individualized review of the totality of the circumstances regarding an applicant's criminal background.

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Disclosure
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating campaign financing, ensuring transparency in political contributions, and enhancing public access to government information.

Document 1: Prohibits an executive agency from requiring an entity that is bidding on a federal contract ... to disclose information on any payments to a candidate for federal office.
as a condition for submitting such contract bid or for participating in the acquisition of property or services.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure.'
Requires any communication transmitted through radio or television to include an individual or organizational disclosure statement.

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections related to credit reporting and financial services.

Document 1: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
authorize a person or the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to furnish to a consumer reporting agency information relating to the performance of a consumer
Document 2: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to extend its coverage to spending card accounts
Requires a financial institution to offer electronic fund transfer services in connection with a spending card account
Document 3: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to exclude from a consumer report
provides a mechanism for veterans to dispute the inclusion of program debt already on a credit report

topic.name: Communications Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at updating and regulating various aspects of communication services and broadcasting rights in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow any cable system or other multichannel video programming distributor to retransmit the signal of a broadcasting station only with the express authority of that station.
Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to commence a rulemaking proceeding to revise regulations governing the exercise by broadcast stations of the right to grant such consent.
Document 2: Prohibits a provider of commercial mobile communications service from receiving universal service support under specified provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 for the provision of such service through the Federal Communications Commission's Lifeline program.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the FCC to require that customer premises equipment provide internal means for effective use with hearing aids that are designed to be compatible with telephones.
States that no action taken by the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to implement this Act shall mandate the use or incorporation of proprietary technology.

topic.name: Military Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving and ensuring benefits for military personnel, veterans, and their families.

Document 1: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation
Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and military retired pay.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to establish a program to provide transportation on Department of Defense (DOD) aircraft
Allows the Secretary to establish an order of priority based on considerations of military needs.
Document 3: Provides for funding the pay and allowances, without interruption, of members of the Armed Forces
including reserve personnel, during any period of lapsed appropriations.

topic.name: Child and Environmental Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety standards for children's products and ensuring safe drinking water by addressing hazardous substances like lead and phthalates.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 2008 to expand the prohibition against the manufacture, distribution, or importation of any children's toy or child care articles containing specified phthalates
Expands also the list of prohibited phthalates.
Document 2: This bill amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require public water systems to notify their customers of lead concentration levels in drinking water that exceed lead limits
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) must notify the public within 15 days of the lead levels exceeding those limits.
Document 3: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992 to set the environmental intervention blood lead level
Requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to amend relevant regulations to comply with this Act within 90 days.

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at improving labor rights and protections for employees, including issues of discrimination, work hours, and collective bargaining.

Document 1: amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to expand exceptions to the prohibition against sex discrimination
Civil penalties are established for violation of prohibitions against sex discrimination.
Document 2: amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to apply its maximum hours requirements to over-the-road bus drivers.
Document 3: amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize

Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.

topic.name: Detention and Interrogation Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures and policies regarding the detention and interrogation of individuals classified as enemy combatants, particularly in relation to Guantanamo Bay.

Document 1: allows an alien detained by the United States as an enemy combatant to apply for a writ of habeas corpus
Limits the review of prosecutions, trials, or judgments of a military commission
Document 2: U.S. Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba is a location for the detention of individuals
prohibition on the use of any federal funds to transfer any individual detained at Guantanamo
Document 3: directs the President to close the detention facility at Guantanamo Bay
requires any interrogation carried out on behalf of the CIA to be conducted only by a CIA employee

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing teacher recruitment, retention, and student protection within the education system.

Document 1: Diverse Teachers Recruitment Act of 2011
recruit individuals from underrepresented groups as public elementary and secondary school teachers
Document 2: Protecting Students from Failing Institutions Act
discharge a borrower's liability on a loan under the Federal Family Education Loan (FFEL) program
Document 3: Teacher Incentive Fund Act
create a Teacher Incentive Fund program of competitive grants to assist states, local educational agencies (LEAs), and nonprofit organizations

topic.name: Government Transparency and Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and public participation in government processes and decision-making.

Document 1: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to repeal the nonapplicability of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) to the Financial Stability Oversight Council
Requires all Council meetings to be open to attendance and participation by members of specified congressional committees.
Document 2: Requires states to accept and process absentee ballots of overseas uniformed services and civilian voters submitted by the voter to a provider of express mail no later than the day before election day
Requires the Presidential designee to reimburse overseas uniformed services voters for the costs of using a provider of express mail services to transmit the ballot to the official.
Document 3: Requires states to carry out congressional redistricting in accordance with a process under which members of the public are informed of redistricting proposals via the Internet
Requires each state redistricting entity to establish and maintain a public Internet site meeting specified requirements.

topic.name: Firearm Regulation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and control of firearms and ammunition in various contexts.

Document 1: prohibit the sale or other disposition of a firearm or ammunition
prevented from boarding an aircraft
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of the Army from promulgating or enforcing any regulation that prohibits possession of a firearm
compliance with the law of the state
Document 3: expand the definition of 'armor piercing ammunition'
require the Attorney General to promulgate standards for the uniform testing of projectiles

topic.name: Airport Security and Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to airport security, aviation services, and infrastructure improvements.

Document 1: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Transportation Security Administration [TSA]) to issue regulations for airport perimeter screening.
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to hire necessary passenger and baggage screeners.
Document 2: Repeals the Essential Air Service Local Participation Program.
Establishes within the Office of the Secretary the Office of Rural Aviation.

topic.name: Food Safety and Labeling Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the safety, inspection, and labeling of food products, including meat, poultry, and dietary supplements.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act and the Poultry Products Inspection Act
authorize the interstate distribution of state inspected meat and poultry
Document 2: Trans Fat Truth in Labeling Act of 2009
require the label of a food that has less than .5 grams of trans fat per serving to express that information
Document 3: Dietary Supplement Labeling Act of 2011
expand the registration requirements for a dietary supplement manufacturing facility

topic.name: Native Community Health and Recognition
topic.description: Legislation focused on the recognition, health improvement, and compensation of Native communities in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages to organize as Urban Corporations
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation
Document 2: Reauthorizes the Native Hawaiian Health Care Improvement Act
Requires Papa Ola Lokahi to report to Congress on the impact of federal and state health care financing mechanisms

topic.name: Legal Protections for Servicemembers
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing legal rights and protections for servicemembers and veterans, particularly regarding employment, education, and healthcare.

Document 1: Amends the sanctions provisions in Rule 11 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
Requires any sanction to compensate parties injured by the conduct in question.
Document 2: Requires the appropriate U.S. district court to use its equity powers
Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) to require an institution of higher education to provide a credit or refund to the servicemember.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from excluding from coverage under traumatic injury provisions
Makes such amendment retroactive to the establishment of such coverage.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Policies
topic.description: Policies and regulations governing the operations, employee management, and funding initiatives of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: ensuring reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees
consultation process for changes in pay policies and schedules
Document 2: special postage stamp for first-class mail that costs not less than 15% more
funding for breast cancer research
Document 3: purchase or lease of low emission and fuel efficient vehicles
prohibits USPS from reducing mail delivery frequency or closing post offices

topic.name: Financial Crime and Law Enforcement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at addressing financial crimes and enhancing law enforcement capabilities in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010-FY2012 to the U.S. Secret Service, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the Department of Justice (DOJ), and other federal agencies for the investigation and prosecution of criminal activity involving computers, crimes of fraud.
financial crimes, including mortgage fraud, securities fraud, and financial institution fraud.
Document 2: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 to revise requirements for activities eligible for community development block grant (CDBG) assistance.
Increases from 15% to 25% the limitation on the amount of CDBG assistance that may be used to provide public services.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at regulating abortion practices and protecting the rights of minors and survivors of abortion procedures.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: Directs each state that makes a Medicaid payment from federal funds during the fiscal year for any items or services furnished by an abortion provider to report
publish the report on a public Internet website of the state
Document 3: require any health care practitioner who is present when a child is born alive following an abortion to exercise the same degree of care
subject to criminal prosecution for murder

topic.name: Public Health and Emergency Preparedness
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing public health infrastructure and emergency preparedness through education, professional roles, and organizational support.

Document 1: establish and expand emergency preparedness pipelines
prepare students for public health emergencies
Document 2: National Nurse for Public Health
increase public safety and emergency preparedness

topic.name: Military Accountability and Prevention
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving accountability and prevention measures within the military regarding hazing and sexual misconduct.

Document 1: establish a data-collection system for reports of hazing incidents
improve service member training to better recognize, prevent, and respond to hazing
Document 2: modifies the process for determining whether to try by court-martial a member accused of certain UCMJ offenses
requires courts-martial determinations to be made by a commissioned officer outside the chain of command
Document 3: investigate allegations of retaliatory personnel actions regarding protected communications
instances of rape, sexual assault, or other forms of sexual misconduct in violation of the Uniform Code of Military Justice

topic.name: District of Columbia Legislation
topic.description: Legislative measures and programs related to the governance and administration of the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Amends the District of Columbia Code to direct the Mayor of the District of Columbia
provide educational assistance to members of the District of Columbia National Guard
Document 2: Cedes the District of Columbia to Maryland after Maryland's acceptance of such retrocession
Maintains the exclusive legislative authority and control of Congress over the Area
Document 3: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
appropriates, out of any moneys of the District of Columbia government not otherwise appropriated

topic.name: Trade Enforcement and Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on enforcing trade laws, particularly regarding antidumping and countervailing duties, and promoting domestic manufacturing through regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the Commissioner responsible for U.S. Customs and Border Protection to initiate an investigation into claims of evasion of antidumping or countervailing duties.
Requires the Commissioner to take appropriate additional enforcement measures.
Document 2: This bill amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to determine an individual countervailable subsidy rate for an exporter or producer.
The bill shall apply to any investigation or review initiated by such authority on or after March 1, 2015.
Document 3: Eliminates certain waivers of Buy American requirements that the Secretary of Transportation obligate federal funding for capital public transportation projects only if U.S. steel, iron, and manufactured goods are used in such projects.

topic.name: U.S. Development Assistance and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative efforts and policies aimed at enhancing development assistance and economic recovery, particularly in Haiti and the Caribbean region.

Document 1: establish an interagency mechanism to improve coordination of U.S. development programs with private sector investment activities
ensure that independent analyses of constraints to growth are conducted
Document 2: call the White House Conference on Haiti
make fundamental policy recommendations on ways to assist in Haiti's recovery and long-term development
Document 3: extend the date by which the President must determine whether Haiti is meeting specified political, economic, social, and security requirements

topic.name: Education and Family Leave Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving educational access and family leave rights for employees in the United States.

Document 1: ensure that all students have equitable access to a high-quality, well-rounded education
promote programs that encourage parental and community involvement
Document 2: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993
allows an employee covered by FMLA to take up to 4 hours during any 30-day period of parental involvement leave
Document 3: entitle an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave during any 12-month period because of the death of a son or daughter
Allows such an employee to substitute any available paid leave for any leave without pay

topic.name: Environmental Financial Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on financial incentives and regulations for environmental conservation and renewable energy initiatives.

Document 1: tax credit for investment in qualified renewable school energy bonds
purchase and installation of renewable energy products for public school
Document 2: prohibit the Office of Surface Mining and Reclamation Enforcement from accepting new self-bonds for coal reclamation
minimize the financial liability to the federal or state government
Document 3: treatment of qualified forest conservation bonds as tax-exempt facility bonds
95 percent or more of the net proceeds of such bonds to be used for specified forestry project costs

topic.name: Disability and Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal legislative efforts aimed at preventing discrimination based on disability, sexual orientation, and gender identity, as well as regulating marriage-related judicial authority.

Document 1: Amends the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 to prohibit the Attorney General from administering or enforcing certain accessibility regulations relating to pools.
Prohibits regulations added under final rules issued by the AG entitled: 'Nondiscrimination on the Basis of Disability in State and Local Government Services'.
Document 2: Amends the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to prohibit discrimination on account of sexual orientation or gender identity when extending credit.
Defines 'gender identity' as the gender-related identity, appearance, or mannerisms or other gender-related characteristics of an individual.

topic.name: Commemoration and Honor Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the establishment of commissions and permissions for honoring significant individuals and events in U.S. history.

Document 1: Establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission to honor Ronald Reagan on the occasion of the 100th anniversary of his birth
Establishes the Star-spangled Banner and War of 1812 Bicentennial Commission to commemorate historic events associated with the War of 1812.
Document 2: Permits statues honoring deceased citizens of U.S. territories to be placed in Statuary Hall of the U.S. Capitol.
Limits such statues to one per jurisdiction.

topic.name: Taxation and Regulation of Beverages
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the taxation and regulation of alcoholic beverages, including wine, mead, and distilled spirits.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to require mead and low alcohol by volume wine to be taxed at the same rate as still wines
contains less than 8.5% alcohol by volume.
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the excise tax on medical device manufacturers and importers.
Document 3: allows taxpayers who are liable for not more than $50,000 per year in excise taxes on distilled spirits, wine, or beer to file and pay such taxes quarterly
Makes this Act effective 90 days after its enactment.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to U.S. foreign policy initiatives focusing on education, humanitarian issues, and human rights in specific countries.

Document 1: Establishes the Vietnam Education Foundation within the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs of the Department of State.
Authorizes the Secretary of State to award grants to institutions of higher education for a partnership with the government of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of State to develop a comprehensive strategy for facilitating the adoption of North Korean children by U.S. citizens.
Document 3: Prohibits FY2014 or FY2015 funds from being made available for security assistance to the government of Burma unless the Secretary of State certifies to Congress.
Directs the Secretary of State to report annually to Congress on the status of military-to-military engagement between the U.S. Armed Forces and the Burmese military.

topic.name: Public Health Research and Policy
topic.description: Legislation focused on research and policy initiatives to improve public health outcomes related to vaccination, tick-borne diseases, and viral hepatitis.

Document 1: comprehensive study to compare total health outcomes, including the risk of autism, between vaccinated and unvaccinated U.S. populations
determine whether vaccines or vaccine components play a role in the development of autism spectrum or other neurological conditions.
Document 2: establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee
ensure interagency coordination and communication regarding efforts to address tick-borne diseases.
Document 3: develop, implement, and update a plan for the prevention, control, and medical management of hepatitis B and hepatitis C
support state, local, territorial, and tribal efforts to provide counseling and education on hepatitis B and hepatitis C.

topic.name: Internet and Electronic Commerce Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating internet access, electronic communications privacy, and financial aspects of energy resource revenues.

Document 1: permanent ban on state and local taxation of Internet access
discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce
Document 2: prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to divulge contents of communications
requirements for governmental entities to provide notice to customers
Document 3: direct the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit qualified outer Continental Shelf revenues
financial assistance to states from OCS revenues

topic.name: Gas Pipeline and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving the safety and environmental standards of gas pipeline operations and occupational safety within the energy sector.

Document 1: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking or may pose high risk of leaking.

Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines for identifying and classifying high-risk pipeline infrastructure.
Document 2: This bill amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 to prohibit the assessment of penalties against employers for non-willful, unrepeated, or non-serious violations of occupational safety and health standards.
No penalty shall be assessed for a violation if it shall be abated by the employer in accordance with the citation or order before the end of that period.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to implement a deepwater technologies research and development program, which includes addressing technology challenges for well control and accident prevention.
Directs the Secretary to ensure the safe and environmentally responsible production of natural gas and other petroleum resources of the United States.

topic.name: Employment and Unemployment Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating unemployment benefits, public transportation compensation, and employment assistance programs.

Document 1: Prohibits a public transportation agency that receives federal funds from providing annual compensation...
exceeds the annual compensation of the President of the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account...
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through January 3, 2013.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow states, for a 120-day period...
to implement an employment assistance voucher program, in lieu of paying unemployment compensation directly to employees.

topic.name: Federal Assistance and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at regulating assistance programs and ensuring compliance in specific sectors such as nutrition, horseracing, and support for disabled veterans.

Document 1: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to make households in which each member receives state assistance eligible for SNAP.
States that any household in which a member receives substantial lottery or gambling winnings shall lose SNAP eligibility immediately.
Document 2: Amends the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978 to prohibit entering a horse in a race if under the influence of a performance-enhancing drug.
Prohibits a host racing association from conducting a race unless it has in place a policy that bans providing a performance-enhancing drug.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Foreign Relations Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the support and designation of military allies, immigration provisions for Afghan allies, and employment initiatives for veterans.

Document 1: Terminates the Islamic Republic of Pakistan's designation as a major non-NATO ally.
Directs the President to submit an annual report to Congress containing a list of each country designated as a major non-NATO ally.
Document 2: Amends the Afghan Allies Protection Act of 2009 to increase the number of Afghan special immigrant visas available.
Extend unused visa carryover authority through December 31, 2016.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the federal government should support private sector employment initiatives for military personnel.
Authorizes the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to award grants to nonprofit organizations to assist individuals in securing employment.

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Relations and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies governing immigration, trade, and military presence between the United States and Cuba.

Document 1: Increases to five years the period during which a Cuban national must be physically present in the United States in order to adjust to permanent resident status.
Makes an alien who returns to Cuba after admission or parole into the United States ineligible for such status adjustment.
Document 2: This bill repeals or amends current laws restricting trade with Cuba.
The prohibition on assistance to Cuba, and the President's authority for the embargo on Cuba, under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 are eliminated.

topic.name: Census and Religious Data Privacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating the collection and use of demographic data, particularly in relation to religious affiliation and ethnic descent, during the U.S. census process.

Document 1: prohibits U.S. government officials from collecting information or utilizing a registry or database to classify on the basis of religious affiliation
prohibits studies related to the collection of such information
Document 2: include in any questionnaire used in a decennial census to determine state populations an option for respondents to indicate Dominican extraction or descent
Document 3: Declares matter that bears the term 'census' on its envelope... to be nonmailable by the United States Postal Service

topic.name: Coal Industry Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to coal mining, environmental regulations, and water resource management in the context of reclamation and employee benefits.

Document 1: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
transfers specified excess funds derived from coal mine operator-paid reclamation fees to the trustees of the 1974 United Mine Workers of America (UMWA) Pension Plan
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
excludes from such definition: the disposal of excess spoil material resulting from coal surface mining and reclamation activities in waters of the United States
Document 3: Amends the Colorado River Storage Project Act
rehabilitate a water delivery system to conserve water or to improve environmental conditions associated with an existing reclamation project

topic.name: Nuclear Safety and Compensation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and measures related to the safety of nuclear activities, environmental impacts, and compensation for individuals affected by radiation exposure.

Document 1: projects for bank stabilization on the eastern shore of Crewboat Cut to prevent erosion from wave action and scouring.
Document 2: extends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Trust Fund until 19 years after enactment of this Act.
increases the amount of compensation an individual filing a claim may receive to $150,000.
Document 3: any action having as a purpose the resumption of testing of one or more nuclear weapons
requires a detailed environmental impact statement in conformance with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969.

topic.name: Disaster Relief and Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support and liability protections for individuals and businesses affected by disasters.

Document 1: Good Samaritan Health Professionals Act of 2011
a health care professional shall not be liable under federal or state law for harm caused by any act or omission if the professional is serving as a volunteer for purposes of responding to a disaster
Document 2: appropriates funds to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to provide disaster relief funds
assist fisherman and small businesses which have suffered economic hardship due to the shutdown of the commercial Dungeness and rock crab season
Document 3: Rural Community Flood Protection Act of 2010
conduct a levee system evaluation and certification for purposes of the National Flood Insurance Program

topic.name: Judicial System Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes aimed at restructuring the federal judicial system, including the appointment of judges and modifications to court jurisdictions.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to divide the Ninth Judicial Circuit into the Ninth Circuit and the Twelfth Circuit.
Requires the President to appoint five additional circuit judges for the new Ninth Circuit.
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint four additional district judges for the eastern district of California.
Makes Bakersfield an additional place of holding court for the eastern district.
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law to permit an individual debtor to exempt from the property of the estate in bankruptcy the debtor's aggregate interest in firearms.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments and regulations affecting transportation, taxation, and healthcare coding standards.

Document 1: Amends Federal highway law to authorize the State of West Virginia to allow, by special permit, the operation of vehicles with a gross vehicle weight of up to 126,000 pounds for the hauling of coal and coal by-products.
on Interstate Route 77 in Kanawha County, West Virginia, between Exits 85 and 97.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax deduction for qualified tuition and related expenses.
Document 3: Protecting Patients and Physicians Against Coding Act of 2015
HHS shall also give these practitioners assistance during the grace period.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Immigration and Nationality Act concerning nonimmigrant and immigrant visa regulations for nationals from specific countries and employment-based immigration.

Document 1: Makes New Zealand nationals eligible to enter the United States as nonimmigrant traders and investors
if New Zealand provides reciprocal nonimmigrant treatment to U.S. nationals.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the per-country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants
establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000 plus the previous year's unused visas.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to extend for two years the Secretary of Homeland Security's authority to waive the low nonimmigrant visa refusal rate requirement
under the visa waiver program.

topic.name: Military and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at balancing military operations with environmental considerations, particularly in relation to renewable energy and weather preparedness.

Document 1: Protection of Military Airfields from Wind Turbine Encroachment Act
make new wind facilities located near certain military installations ineligible for tax credits
Document 2: Clean Energy Jobs and American Power Act
provisions concerning the reduction of global warming pollution, energy efficiency, renewable energy
Document 3: Weather Alerts for a Ready Nation Act of 2015
directs the National Weather Service to employ warning coordination meteorologists

topic.name: Health Policy and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various health-related legislative acts aimed at improving care and management of chronic diseases and conditions.

Document 1: accelerate the development of treatments that would prevent, halt, or reverse the course of Alzheimer's
coordinate the health care and treatment of citizens with Alzheimer's
Document 2: study and report to Congress on the suitability and feasibility of designating prehistoric, historic, and limestone forest sites
Document 3: review uptake and utilization of the Medicare diabetes screening benefit
maximize economies of scale, cost-effectiveness, and resource allocation in increasing utilization of the diabetes screening benefit

topic.name: Economic and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting economic development and environmental conservation.

Document 1: Reinstates provisions of the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990
allow military departments to transfer surplus real and personal property for economic development purposes
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to determine mortgage credit availability
increase the limitation on the maximum original principal obligation of a mortgage
Document 3: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act
directs the Secretary of Commerce to urge international fishery management organizations to adopt shark conservation measures

topic.name: Legal Protections for Individuals
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at safeguarding the rights and freedoms of individuals in various contexts, including military personnel and victims of domestic abuse.

Document 1: Requires the Armed Forces to accommodate the conscience and sincerely held moral principles and religious beliefs of its members
Prohibits any member from directing, ordering, or requiring a chaplain to perform any duty, rite, service, or function that is contrary to his or her beliefs.
Document 3: Prohibits an insurer from denying or terminating an insurance policy or health benefit plan on the basis that the insured is the subject of domestic abuse
Requires insurers to develop and adhere to written policies specifying procedures to be followed to protect the safety and privacy of a subject of abuse.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at modifying health insurance coverage and financial assistance related to healthcare costs.

Document 1: health insurance and group health plans to cover, as preventive care for women, over-the-counter oral contraceptives
Insurers and plans cannot impose cost sharing for preventive care.
Document 2: no refundable premium assistance tax credits for coverage under a qualified health plan
Amends PPACA to exclude collectively bargained plans from the meaning of 'qualified health plan.'
Document 3: exclude any amount includible in gross income for converting to a Roth Individual Retirement Account
the amount of the tax credit for health care insurance premium assistance.

topic.name: Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving regulatory processes and standards in different sectors, including healthcare and veterans' recognition.

Document 1: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to establish a third-party quality system assessment program
Document 2: adds National Korean War Veterans Armistice Day
to the list of days on which the flag of the United States should be displayed
Document 3: amends federal criminal code provisions prohibiting sex trafficking of children
to eliminate the requirement that the defendant had a reasonable opportunity to observe the victim

topic.name: Insurance Producer Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the licensing and regulation of independent insurance producers across multiple states.

Document 1: Access to Independent Health Insurance Advisors Act of 2012
excludes remuneration paid for licensed independent insurance producers from administrative cost calculations
Document 2: National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers Reform Act of 2008
reestablishes the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers as a nonprofit corporation
Document 3: National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers Reform Act of 2011
authorizes NARAB to establish membership criteria, including a mandatory criminal background check for state-licensed insurance producers

topic.name: Energy Development on Indian Lands
topic.description: Legislation and policies governing energy exploration and development on Native American lands.

Document 1: This bill amends the Energy Policy Act of 1992 to allow the Department of the Interior, an affected Indian tribe, or a certified third-party appraiser under contract with the Indian tribe to appraise Indian land or trust assets involved in a transaction requiring Interior approval.
Each agency within Interior involved in the review of oil and gas activities on Indian lands must use a uniform system of reference numbers and tracking systems for oil and gas wells.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands.
Document 3: Amends the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2008 to repeal its prohibition against the use of funds to publish regulations regarding a commercial leasing program for oil shale resources on public lands.

topic.name: Healthcare Payment Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at reforming payment methodologies and quality measures within healthcare systems, particularly focusing on Medicare and Medicaid services.

Document 1: removes sustainable growth rate (SGR) methodology from the determination of annual conversion factors
establish a Merit-based Incentive Payment (MIP) system
Document 2: make demonstration project grants for the expansion of state registries
study and report on the demonstration projects
Document 3: require payment under the Medicaid program for services furnished by recipients of Ryan White Part C grants
subject to specified quality management and reporting requirements

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks for Trade and Privacy
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at regulating trade practices, consumer protection, and privacy rights in the context of controlled substances, unfair trade practices, and student data privacy.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that...
evidence that a substance was not marketed, advertised, or labeled for human consumption shall not by itself be sufficient...
Document 2: This bill amends the Tariff Act of 1930, with respect to unfair practices in the import trade...
the ITC may determine during an investigation that exclusion of the articles concerned from entry into the United States would not be in the public interest...
Document 3: Prohibits an operator of a school's Internet or online service...
Requires operators to disclose publicly the types of personal information they collect or generate...

topic.name: Concussion Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the prevention, treatment, and research of concussions, particularly in youth sports and educational settings.

Document 1: Requires the CDC to evaluate existing surveillance and data collection systems that track the incidence and circumstances of traumatic brain injury, including concussion.
Ensure that the System is designed in a manner that facilitates further research on brain injury.
Document 2: Requires each state educational agency to issue regulations establishing minimum requirements for the prevention and treatment of concussions.
Prohibits a student who has sustained a concussion from resuming participation until a health care professional provides a written release.
Document 3: Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission to determine standards regarding youth football helmets and concussion resistance.
To initiate a rulemaking proceeding for a consumer product safety rule regarding standards for youth football helmets.

topic.name: Federal Grant and Regulatory Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to improve the federal grant-making process, enhance transparency, and promote regulatory changes in various sectors, including health and environmental protection.

Document 1: Requires the President to establish the National Commission on Earmark Reform
Requires all federal grants, both formula and discretionary, from all grant making agencies to be posted on Grants.gov.
Document 2: Requires the Food and Drug Administration to issue guidance that addresses using alternative statistical methods
in the development and review of drugs and biological products.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should negotiate with the other parties to the Montreal Protocol
Amends the Clean Air Act to allow the production and consumption of class II substances that are used as fire suppression agents.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating specific areas such as military canine welfare, labor practices, and pharmaceutical competition.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to classify military working dogs as canine members of the Armed Forces.
Requires that such dogs no longer be classified as equipment.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide that nothing in specified prohibitions against unfair labor practices by employers shall be construed as requiring an employer to employ any person.
in furtherance of other employment or agency status.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug.
until the expiration or forfeiture of the exclusivity period granted to the generic manufacturer.

topic.name: Land Use and Military Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to land management, environmental preservation, and military personnel policies affecting land use and discharge procedures.

Document 1: adjusts the boundary of the Santa Monica Mountains National Recreation Area
acquire only through exchange, donation, or purchase from a willing seller
Document 2: allows portions of the former bombardment area on the island of Culebra, Puerto Rico, to be utilized for purposes that would require decontamination
utilization is prohibited
Document 3: prohibits the Secretary of a military department from discharging due to a personality disorder
completion by the Secretary of Defense of a review of Department of Defense policies and procedures

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative acts aimed at establishing and protecting rights, whether related to human rights, environmental conservation, or the rights of the unborn.

Document 1: Mandating America's Responsibility To Limit Abuse, Negligence and Depravity Act
human rights violations, including child abuse, shall not be conducted or condoned on any U.S. military installation
Document 2: Gulf of the Farallones and Cordell Bank National Marine Sanctuaries Boundary Modification and Protection Act
U.S. policy to protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
Document 3: Life at Conception Act of 2016
the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human being at all stages of life

topic.name: Legislative Support for Vulnerable Groups
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and support to vulnerable populations, including veterans and individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Housing Assistance for Veterans Act of 2013
rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 3: IDEA MOE Adjustment Act
limit the penalty imposed on a state that reduces its level of funding for special education

topic.name: Environmental and Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting public safety and the environment from hazardous substances and pollution.

Document 1: Bans as a hazardous substance and prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution in commerce of jewelry containing cadmium, barium, or antimony for children 12 years old or younger.
Sets forth factors to determine whether jewelry is designed or intended for wear or use by such children.
Document 2: This bill amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to make foreign facilities that are located offshore and outside the exclusive economic zone (EEZ) liable for removal costs and damages that result from oil spills.
Specifically, the following parties may be held liable: the owners or operators of the foreign facilities.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and transparency within government operations, particularly regarding whistleblower protections and the management of public records.

Document 1: This bill revises whistle-blower protections for a Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) employee or job applicant who discloses wrongdoing to an appropriate official.
the bill sets forth procedures for filing, investigating, adjudicating, and reviewing whistle-blower retaliation complaints.
Document 2: direct the Attorney General to make grants to states and local governments for the complete and accurate recording, by both audio and video means, of every custodial interrogation occurring within the state or unit of local government.
Document 3: requires the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA) to establish the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Collection.

establishes the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Review Board, which shall require all records that relate to the loss, fate, or status of missing Armed Forces personnel to be transmitted to NARA and disclosed to the public.

topic.name: Federal Government Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to federal employee health records, state resource management, and tribal payments.

Document 1: Federal Employees Electronic Personal Health Records Act of 2007
requires establishment and maintenance of electronic personal health records
Document 2: transfer from the Secretary of the Treasury to the state of Colorado
used for conservation, restoration, and protection of land, water, and wildlife resources
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to pay
to the Quapaw Tribe of Oklahoma

topic.name: Government Legislation for Public Services
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving public services, including healthcare, environmental management, and education.

Document 1: Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy
ensure timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy
Document 2: Emerald Ash Borer Revolving Loan Fund
loans to eligible local government units for equipment to monitor, dispose of, and replace infested trees
Document 3: amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act
lower the frequency of standardized testing in schools

topic.name: Tax and Sentencing Reforms
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative amendments related to tax regulations and criminal sentencing reforms, particularly focusing on military provisions and drug offenses.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow penalty-free distributions of amounts from a flexible spending arrangement to members of a military reserve component ordered or called to active duty.
flexible spending arrangement to members of a military reserve component ordered or called to active duty for at least 180 days or for an indefinite period.
Document 2: Fairness in Cocaine Sentencing Act of 2011 - Amends the Controlled Substances Act and the Controlled Substances Import and Export Act to eliminate increased and mandatory minimum penalties for drug offenses involving mixtures or substances which contain cocaine base.
eliminate increased and mandatory minimum penalties for drug offenses involving mixtures or substances which contain cocaine base (i.e., crack cocaine).
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the special rules for the accelerated depreciation of business property used on Indian reservations.
accelerated depreciation of business property used on Indian reservations.

topic.name: Maritime Transportation and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and programs related to maritime transportation, including tax exemptions, promotion of U.S.-flagged vessel usage, and ocean exploration initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the harbor maintenance tax commercial cargo
Defines the 'Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System' as the waterway between Duluth, Minnesota, and Sept Iles, Quebec.
Document 2: Amends the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2006 to expand the directive to the Department of Transportation
Require DOT to give top priority to the processing of a license for liquefied natural gas facilities at a deepwater port.
Document 3: Requires the administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to establish within NOAA a coordinated national ocean exploration program
Requires that the program be conducted through a national headquarters, a network of extramural regional undersea research centers.

topic.name: Water Infrastructure and Conservation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to enhance water infrastructure resilience, conservation, and ecosystem protection.

Document 1: establish the Water Infrastructure Resiliency and Sustainability Program
promote more efficient water use, water conservation, water reuse, or recycling
Document 2: establish a Delaware River Basin restoration program
improve or protect water quality by reducing pollutants and restoring headwater areas

topic.name: Education and Public Health Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving access to education and public health services, particularly for underserved populations.

Document 1: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires the Department of Education to rank institutions of higher education based on the percentage of first-time, full-time students who receive Pell Grants
Document 3: establish within the Public Health Service an Office of the National Nurse
conduct media campaigns and provide guidance for activities that will increase public safety and emergency preparedness

topic.name: Federal Grant Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various federal acts that establish grant programs aimed at enhancing tourism, education, and healthcare services.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a competitive grant program to promote domestic regional tourism growth
Limits grant amounts to a minimum of $100,000 and a maximum of $1 million.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to states to create or approve teacher or principal preparation academies
Requires state grantees to recognize a certificate of completion from an academy as at least the equivalent of a master's degree in education.

topic.name: Health Policy and Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to health care benefits, cancer prevention, and wildlife management as it pertains to public health and safety.

Document 1: no individual who elects to opt out of benefits under part A (Hospital Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare)
opt out of benefits under SSA title II (Old-Age, Survivors, and Disability Insurance)
Document 2: amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services
prostate cancer prevention programs including screening and referrals for treatment
Document 3: prohibits any wolf of the species Canis lupus in Minnesota, Wisconsin, and Michigan
exclusive jurisdiction over the management of those wolves within its borders

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, use, and restrictions of federal lands and resources, including aviation access and environmental compliance.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of any part of any appropriation contained in any Act for print, radio, television or any other media advertisement, campaign, or form of publicity against the use of a food or non-alcoholic beverage that is lawfully marketed under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.
Document 2: Withdraws approximately 512 acres of covered land in Arizona from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Grants the Secretary of the Interior exclusive jurisdiction to manage the Craigin Project in accordance with the Arizona Water Settlements Act.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretaries of the Interior or Agriculture from taking any action that would permanently close, restrict, or render or declare unserviceable any aircraft landing strip located on land under their jurisdiction.
Requires the Secretaries to establish a nationwide policy for governing backcountry aviation issues relating to the management of federal land.
